expressions.name: conv3d_bias_library_or_implicit_gemm
expressions.formula: Semantic formula: X has shape (N,C_in,D_in,H_in,W_in)=(128,3,16,32,32). W_conv has shape (C_out,C_in,Kd,Kh,Kw)=(16,3,3,3,3). B_conv has shape (C_out)=(16). With stride=1, padding=1, dilation=1, groups=1, D_o=16,H_o=32,W_o=32. Y_conv[n,c_o,d_o,h_o,w_o] = B_conv[c_o] + sum_{c_i=0..2} sum_{k_d=0..2} sum_{k_h=0..2} sum_{k_w=0..2} X[n,c_i,d_o+k_d-1,h_o+k_h-1,w_o+k_w-1] * W_conv[c_o,c_i,k_d,k_h,k_w], where out-of-bound X indices contribute 0. Performance-lowering contract for the code generator: this expression is a heavyweight Conv3d+bias stage and must not be translated literally into one Triton program per output element. Preferred lowering is framework/vendor Conv3d (cuDNN) when library calls are allowed. If Triton-only code is required, lower this as an implicit-GEMM Conv3d template with M=N*D_o*H_o*W_o=2097152, Ncol=C_out=16, K=C_in*Kd*Kh*Kw=81, using tiled tl.dot over BLOCK_M x BLOCK_K and BLOCK_K x BLOCK_N tiles. Suggested tile family: BLOCK_M in {32,64}, BLOCK_N=16, BLOCK_K in {32,64}, num_warps=4, num_stages=3. Forbidden lowerings: BLOCK_O=1 scalar Conv3d, one program per single output element, dynamic hot-path loops over C_in/Kd/Kh/Kw, or any naive Conv3d replacement that does not use a tiled library-like template.
expressions.boundary_treatment: Zero padding of one element is applied on D/H/W. Only input coordinates 0<=d<D_in, 0<=h<H_in, 0<=w<W_in contribute; invalid coordinates contribute 0. The performance contract in formula is part of the semantic intent for downstream codegen: preserve PyTorch Conv3d semantics while avoiding scalar-per-output lowering.
expressions.TDD_requirement: Reference is torch.nn.Conv3d(3,16,kernel_size=3,stride=1,padding=1,bias=True) in eval mode. Verify shape (128,16,16,32,32), zero-padding boundary handling, and bias over C_out. Because cuDNN may use TF32 on A100, the test should either align both sides to TF32 or use tolerance no tighter than about rtol=atol=2e-3 for this stage. Also reject generated code that implements this stage as BLOCK_O=1 scalar Conv3d, because such code may pass correctness but is known to be the performance failure mode for this task.

expressions.name: maxpool3d_channel_logsumexp_relu_fused
expressions.formula: Semantic formula: for each final output coordinate (n,d_p,h_p,w_p), first compute P[n,c,d_p,h_p,w_p] = max_{delta_d,delta_h,delta_w in [0,2)} Y_conv[n,c,2*d_p+delta_d,2*h_p+delta_h,2*w_p+delta_w]. Then compute stable channel logsumexp L[n,0,d_p,h_p,w_p] = M + log(sum_{c=0..15} exp(P[n,c,d_p,h_p,w_p]-M)), where M=max_{c=0..15} P[n,c,d_p,h_p,w_p]. Final output Y[n,0,d_p,h_p,w_p] = max(L[n,0,d_p,h_p,w_p],0). Performance-lowering contract for the code generator: this expression intentionally fuses MaxPool3d, channel LogSumExp keepdim=True, and ReLU. Generate one Triton post-processing kernel over final output elements; do not materialize the intermediate pooled tensor P/Y_pool with shape (128,16,8,16,16). Each program should handle a block of final output positions, e.g. BLOCK_ELEMS in {128,256}; for each position vectorize over C_out=16 and pool window volume=8, reduce the 8 pool values with max per channel, reduce the 16 pooled channel values with stable logsumexp, apply ReLU, and store Y. Forbidden lowerings: standalone MaxPool3d kernel followed by standalone LogSumExp/ReLU kernel, any large intermediate Y_pool write/read, or unstable log(sum(exp(x))) without subtracting max.
expressions.boundary_treatment: MaxPool3d uses kernel=2,stride=2,padding=0. The input spatial shape (16,32,32) is exactly divisible by 2, so every pooling window is fully inside Y_conv. LogSumExp covers the complete channel range C_out=16 and must use subtract-max for numerical stability. ReLU clamps negative L values to 0. The fused implementation should preserve keepdim=True, so output channel dimension is 1.
expressions.TDD_requirement: Reference is torch.max_pool3d(Y_conv,kernel_size=2,stride=2) followed by torch.logsumexp(..., dim=1, keepdim=True) and torch.relu. Verify output shape (128,1,8,16,16), exact pooling window mapping, keepdim=True, stable logsumexp, and non-negative final output. Suggested tolerance rtol=atol=1e-5 for this post-processing stage when Y_conv inputs are identical. Also reject generated code that writes a materialized Y_pool tensor or splits this stage into two post-processing kernels, because that is the known avoidable memory/launch overhead.
